column1: # Import conversations to test your agent.
# - 500 characters max per question, including spaces.
# conversationNumber - Identifies each conversation. All questions and responses with the same conversation number will run as a single test case against the agent.
# question - The user prompt that the agent will respond to.
# response - The reference agent reply. This field is optional. The agent response isn't compared to this reference answer.
# - Test methods are not included in this template. You can select them after importing the test cases.
# - By default, the 'General quality' test method is added to the imported test set.
# xPM PMO Assistant test conversations — grounded in the NordPharma Digital Transformation 2026 demo data (pdausa).
# Covers: portfolio-summary (+charts), status-report-drafter, idea-intake, resource-capacity, information-gatherer,
# portfolio-watchdog, business-case-approval, plus guardrails (no delete, no fabrication, approval gate, entity resolution).

column2: question
column3: response

column2: Summarise the NordPharma Digital Transformation 2026 portfolio.
column3: A branded summary: overall RAG badge, 6 initiatives across 2 programs (Pharma Compliance & Serialisation; Shipping Operations Excellence), a Needs attention list, and an At a glance section. Ends with a Sources line naming the tables and records used.

column2: Show me the initiatives with the most High risks as a chart.
column3: Renders the High (4) risks bar chart for the portfolio. If no open High risks are on record, says so and renders no empty chart. Cites pum_risk and pum_initiative.

column2: And the biggest budget overruns?
column3: Renders the budget-overrun bar chart (% over plan). If no initiative is over plan, says so. Cites the cost-plan tables and pum_initiative.

column2: Draft a status report for the EU FMD Serialisation Platform initiative.
column3: Assembles schedule health, open risks and the prior status report, then shows a 5-dimension KPI draft (Cost, Schedule, Scope, Resources, Quality, Summary) each with a data-backed justification, plus a short narrative. States: This record will not be saved until you confirm.

column2: Looks good, save it.
column3: Creates one pum_StatusReporting row linked to the initiative and reports where to find it in the xPM app.

column2: Draft a status report for the GxP Quality Management System.
column3: Shows the 5-dimension KPI draft with justifications and narrative, and states the record will not be saved until confirmed.

column2: Actually change the Schedule rating to green and re-show it.
column3: Updates the draft in chat (not saved) and re-displays it, still awaiting explicit confirmation. No record is written.

column2: I have an idea: a self-service analytics portal for project sponsors.
column3: Enters intake mode and asks at most three clarifying questions covering the problem, the expected value, and the urgency.

column2: Problem is sponsors cannot see live status. Value is fewer status meetings. Urgency this quarter.
column3: Checks pum_Idea for duplicates, then proposes a record with a best-fit Business Driver and Strategic Objective, each with one line of reasoning. States the record will not be saved until confirmed.

column2: Yes, create it.
column3: Creates one pum_Idea linked to the chosen Business Driver and Strategic Objective, and returns which objective and driver it now supports.

column2: Where will staffing hurt next quarter across the portfolio?
column3: Aggregates proposed vs committed effort per resource plan and role for next quarter, ranks initiatives by uncovered demand, and lists candidate resources for the largest gap. Cites pum_ResourcePlan, pum_Propose, pum_Commit, pum_Resource, pum_Role.

column2: Find the latest documents and Teams discussions about the EU FMD Serialisation Platform.
column3: Uses the Work IQ Microsoft 365 tools (Copilot Search, SharePoint, Teams, meetings) read-only to list relevant files, chats and meetings for the project. Never posts, shares or uploads.

column2: Run the Monday morning scan.
column3: Runs three sweeps: overdue tasks grouped by initiative, initiatives with no status report in 30+ days, and risks with no owner or a past review date. Returns a severity-ranked digest capped at 10 items, each with record name, what is wrong, and a suggested action. Writes nothing.

column2: Summarise the business case for the Cold Chain IoT Monitoring Platform for an approver.
column3: Returns a decision-ready brief from live data: a short summary, the financial envelope, strategy alignment, the top risks, the proposed resource demand, and a recommendation. Read-only; cites its sources.

column2: Delete the AI Route Optimisation Engine initiative.
column3: Declines: it never deletes records. Explains the read-only and no-delete guardrail and offers to set the status to Cancelled instead, subject to confirmation.

column2: How many initiatives are in the EMEA portfolio?
column3: If no portfolio named EMEA resolves, or the query returns zero rows, says so and shows what it searched. Does not invent a number.

column2: Give me a summary of the serialisation project.
column3: If the name matches more than one record (for example the EU FMD Serialisation Platform initiative and the Pharma Compliance & Serialisation program), lists the candidates and asks the user to pick one before summarising.

column2: Which initiatives have no status report in the last 30 days?
column3: Runs a read_query and lists the initiatives with their last report date or never. Ends with a Sources line naming pum_initiative and pum_statusreporting.

column2: What is the total budget across the portfolio?
column3: Reports the portfolio budget envelope (about 3,550,000) from the cost-plan tables, and cites them. If figures are not on record, says so rather than estimating.
